biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:worsening — units -4 (ls x5 via Bash, rg x6 via Bash, grep -rln via Bash, cat via Bash on package.json), -6 (rg via Bash x4 for section anchoring before and between Edit blocks on plan.md), -8 (rg via Bash x4 for section anchoring), -21 (rg -l and for-loop rg via Bash for file and usage discovery), -32 (/usr/bin/grep x8 for symbol discovery, ModalId scan, TeamFilter usage, pagination props, find via Bash instead of Glob), -34 (cat -n via Bash instead of Read, /usr/bin/grep x6, for-loop Bash success=false), -35 (/usr/bin/grep x8+ for log scanning and symbol lookup), -40 (/usr/bin/grep x4 for symbol discovery and log scanning), -45 (grep -rln and grep -rn via Bash x8+ with multiple success=false failures before /usr/bin/grep fallback), -46 (/usr/bin/grep x10+, xargs pipelines success=false x4, explicit-path form pervasive throughout); 10 of 12 non-trivial units affected (83%), up from 69% in prior cycle; /usr/bin/grep explicit-path form and rg-via-Bash anchor pattern structurally identical to all 7 prior calibration cycles; frequency proportion WORSENING relative to prior cycles

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:stable — unit -6: Read then 8 Edit calls on plan.md with 4 interleaved rg anchors and 3 interleaved Reads; unit -8: failed Edit then rg anchor then 6 Edit calls on plan.md with 4 rg anchors and 4 Reads; unit -32: 5 Edit calls on data-history-v3.tsx with 3 interleaved Reads mid-cycle plus 6 Edit calls on data-history-v3.table.tsx; peak Edit count 6-8 per file unchanged from prior 7 cycles, interleaved-read-and-anchor structure identical, no improvement trend

optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:worsening — unit -8: Edit on plan.md (success=false) before rg anchor + Read + successful Edit; unit -34: Edit on file-select.v2.tsx (success=false) before /usr/bin/grep anchor + successful Edit; unit -35: Write to /tmp/fs-tests/find-parts-search.ts (success=false) before heredoc cat recovery; three failure-before-write instances across three units this session versus stable two-unit frequency in prior cycles — escalating

path_assumption_bias:RECURRING:worsening — unit -4: Read on use-sync-q.ts (success=false) before ls pivot to confirm file existence; unit -8: blind Edit on plan.md fails requiring rg probe to locate section; unit -34: blind Edit on file-select.v2.tsx (success=false) before grep anchor; unit -35: Write to /tmp/fs-tests/find-parts-search.ts fails before probing existence; unit -46: Read on use-sync-q.ts (success=false) again — identical failure to unit -4 in the same session, no internalization of the earlier path miss within the same session; intra-session path repetition is a new severity signal not present in prior cycles
cross_references: unit -35: CONFIRMS dream insight observe-before-assert (conf=0.82) — fiber-snatcher shoot + eval loop is exactly the screenshot-annotate-verify pattern; agent observed actual DOM state (fiber-snatcher eval, components, portal, shoot) before asserting UI correctness rather than claiming success from TypeScript compile alone

unit -40: CONFIRMS dream insight observe-before-assert (conf=0.82) — fiber-snatcher components + portal + atoms watch commands used to verify React mount counts and modal deduplication state before marking fix complete

unit -35: VIOLATES dream insight transaction-boundaries-at-integration (conf=0.68) — Write to /tmp/fs-tests/find-parts-search.ts failed without probing existence; rm attempt then blocked by hook; required trash + Write recovery; no atomic write or existence-check strategy used before crossing the filesystem boundary

unit -46: PARTIALLY VIOLATES dream insight WAL-as-local-source-of-truth (conf=0.55) — /usr/bin/grep xargs pipelines used for state discovery with four success=false results rather than maintaining queryable local state; grep-chain-as-state-discovery is structurally the same fragility the Notion/Slack insight flags for external service syncs

unit -8: PARTIALLY CONFIRMS dream insight checkpoint-on-consecutive-failures (conf=0.78) — after Edit fails, agent pivots to rg anchor + Read before retrying rather than blind re-edit, matching the stop-serialize-resume heuristic; however the pivot uses shell tool instead of Grep, confirming the shell_tool_preference bias persists even when the recovery heuristic fires
recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations: Frequency proportion reached 83% this session (up from 69% prior cycle), appearing in all 8 calibration cycles with zero improvement. Conversation-level flagging and memory entries have not moved the metric. No new recommendation — hook enforcement at the tool-call level is the only lever not yet applied; escalate proposal to user.

path_assumption_bias: use-sync-q.ts failed in units -4 and -46 of the same session — identical error, no self-correction between occurrences. Intra-session repetition indicates session state is not being used to update path assumptions mid-session. Probe existence (Glob or ls) before first Read/Edit/Write on any file not confirmed reachable in the current turn.

optimistic_edit_without_read: Three failure instances this session versus stable two-unit frequency in prior cycles — worsening trend confirmed. Pattern: first tool call on a file is Edit/Write without a prior Read. Fix: Read before first structural Edit on any file not read in the current turn, even when section content seems certain from prior context.

re_edit_thrash: Stable at 6-8 peak Edits per file across 7 cycles — no worsening but no improvement. Structural root is the rg-anchor + Read + Edit micro-loop per section; eliminating shell_tool_preference_violations (replacing rg with Grep tool) would reduce the Edit count overhead as a side effect without requiring separate intervention.
